medications.indication_rationale: Injections cannot be given via telehealth, and analgesia is not the priority when meningitis/SAH have not been excluded in a confused patient with a severe new headache.
Standard analgesia has already failed and does not address the need to exclude serious causes; do not exceed doses.
No role in the acute exclusion of intracranial emergencies; may be discussed later for prophylaxis under GP care.

allied_health_referrals.rationale: Neurology/immunology review once serious causes are excluded, for mast cell and headache management.

integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Comfort measure while arranging urgent assessment; not a substitute for exclusion of serious causes.
No evidence for acute management; not recommended.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Flatmate to watch for neck stiffness, fever, projectile vomiting, one-sided weakness/speech change, or worsening confusion/drowsiness, and call 000 if any occur.
Rest in a dark, quiet, cool room and avoid screens while awaiting urgent assessment.
Do not take further painkillers beyond safe limits; have the flatmate manage medications given the confusion.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Prevents medication error in a confused patient.

patient_education_points.point: A severe headache like this, especially with feeling confused, has to be checked in person urgently to make sure it isn't meningitis or a bleed on the brain — even though it may well be a mast cell flare.
Painkiller injections aren't the answer over a chat, and the tablets you've taken haven't helped — the priority is getting you assessed today.

Your flatmate should stay with you and call 000 immediately if your neck becomes stiff so you can't touch chin to chest, you vomit forcefully, one side goes weak, your speech changes, or you become more confused or hard to wake.
In the meantime rest in the dark and quiet — but this is about getting checked, not just managing the pain at home.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent same-day in-person assessment (± CT/LP); neurology/immunology follow-up once serious causes excluded
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Neurological status/meningism, imaging if indicated, mast cell work-up and headache prophylaxis
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Thunderclap onset, neck stiffness, fever, vomiting, focal deficit or worsening consciousness — call 000.

must_include_items: Run the headache red-flag screen (thunderclap, neck stiffness, fever, focal deficit, consciousness)
Arrange urgent in-person assessment for a severe new headache with confusion (± CT/LP)
Do NOT dismiss it on the mast cell label or arrange remote injections
acceptable_alternatives: Direct 000 if any red-flag feature is present
Comfort measures while arranging urgent assessment
errors_of_omission: No urgent assessment for confusion + severe headache
errors_of_commission: Attributing everything to a mast cell flare and managing at home
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires (1) a headache red-flag screen, (2) urgent in-person assessment for the severe headache + confusion, and (3) explicit meningitis/SAH safety-netting. Calling it 'just a mast cell headache' and treating remotely is a fail.
